visualGoal: Make a sim racer who already has FFB understand tactile is the next, high-ROI layer — and give them a wire-it-yourself confidence. This is a low-product-density how-to: ONE verified tactile product (ButtKicker Gamer PRO) carries the visual; everything else leans on wiring/stack TABLES and tip/forum callouts. Do NOT force product cards to hit a visual count.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Dayton Audio BST-1 tactile bass shaker
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The canonical budget DIY transducer (~$30, 25W). Currently text-only + Amazon search in the stack table. Would need a real, license-clean photo before it could become a body visual OR be added to verified-product-images.json. NOT required for this article to ship.
Representative budget amp for the DIY route (~$50). Text-only. Not required to ship.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic sim-racing cockpit under-seat view at dramatic amber rim light, an aluminum-profile seat rail with a rubber-isolated mounting point left open for a real tactile transducer product overlay, a small class-D amplifier on a shelf with a single speaker-wire run, clean cable management, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake transducer, warped hardware, impossible wiring, unreadable text, procedural gradient art, exposed live wires

infographicPrompts.title: PC to amp to shaker: the tactile signal chain
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center flow diagram, large readable labels, racing colors, real transducer photo as the end node for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Which SimHub effects to turn on first
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered ladder, frequency-band motif, no fake product art

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult Gen X sim-racing curator Duke Alvarez crouched beside a cockpit seat rail, one hand on a rubber-isolated transducer mount, confident mechanic energy, small amplifier and tidy wiring visible, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.item: SimHub ShakeIt Bass Shakers — Sound Output panel
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.notes: A real SimHub UI screenshot showing the Sound Output device selection + effect list would materially help the wiring section. Must be a genuine capture (or clearly-labeled annotated capture), not AI. Optional — the wiring TABLE already carries the steps without it.

openQuestions: Standardize ButtKicker Gamer PRO price: official $349.95 vs on-site spec card ~$200 street?
Do we ever want to add Dayton BST-1 / a budget amp to the product catalog + verified images so the DIY route gets a real card? (Would require license-clean photos.)
